method_atom: An exactly solvable equilibrium model of many two-state units coupled pairwise along the links of a random network with an arbitrary connection-degree distribution, exhibiting a symmetry-breaking order-disorder transition at a critical value of a control parameter. The moments of the degree distribution govern the critical behavior: a divergent fourth moment produces anomalous singularities in the collective response and fluctuation quantities, while a divergent second moment drives the critical point to infinity, converts the transition to infinite order, and amplifies finite-size effects.
object_atom: Ferromagnetic Ising spins with nearest-neighbor coupling placed on the vertices of an 'equilibrium' random graph with a prescribed degree distribution P(k), characterized by the critical temperature, magnetization, magnetic susceptibility, and specific heat.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the exactly-solved pairwise-coupled binary-unit model on a heavy-tailed random network whose degree-distribution moments control the critical transition; the domain-specific part is the magnetic Ising spin system and its thermodynamic observables.
